target.title: feat(skills): add watch-cli
target.body:
Adds a \`watch-cli\` skill under \`skills/watch-cli/\`.

## What it is

[watch-cli](https://github.com/sonpiaz/watch-cli) is an MIT-licensed CLI that composes \`yt-dlp\` + \`ffmpeg\` + a Whisper-class ASR into a single command. It hands OpenClaw the raw materials to reason over any social video: VIDEO + evenly-spaced FRAMES + TRANSCRIPT, in a versioned schema.

## Frontmatter

Uses the native OpenClaw frontmatter shape per [\`docs/plugins/manifest.md\`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/plugins/manifest.md):

- \`name: watch-cli\`
- \`description: <locked pitch>\`
- \`homepage: …\`
- \`metadata.openclaw.requires.bins: [watch]\`
- \`metadata.openclaw.install\`: curl-based one-line install

No runtime entrypoint in the manifest — the agent invokes \`watch\` as a subprocess and parses the v1 JSON output (or text fallback) per [\`docs/output-schema.md\`](https://github.com/sonpiaz/watch-cli/blob/main/docs/output-schema.md).

## Why it fits OpenClaw

Pairs with research / coding workflows. Common pattern: user shares a YouTube tutorial / LinkedIn talk / TikTok demo, OpenClaw calls \`watch <url>\`, reads frames as images and transcript as text, then produces a concrete artifact (architecture diagram, React component, runnable notebook, cheat sheet) via one of the 5 prompt templates in [\`prompts/\`](https://github.com/sonpiaz/watch-cli/tree/main/prompts).

## Install (after this PR merges, OpenClaw users run)

\`\`\`bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sonpiaz/watch-cli/main/install.sh | bash
\`\`\`

Plus your ASR backend (default Kyma, or BYOK Groq, or fully offline whisper.cpp — \`.env.example\` documents).

## Supported platforms

YouTube, X, LinkedIn, TikTok, Reddit, Vimeo, Facebook. Login-walled posts fall back to the user's signed-in browser cookies automatically.

MIT licensed. No telemetry. Sub-spec docs: \`docs/output-schema.md\`, \`docs/exit-codes.md\`, \`docs/offline-mode.md\`.
target.author: sonpiaz
